findings.evidence: Lines 63-67 build `hspace` solely as `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_comp (hg.isPositiveDefiniteKernel_sub hstar) Prod.snd`; `SemigroupGroup.lean:170-193` exposes `IsSemigroupGroupPD` directly through the kernel `F (p.1 + q.1, p.2 - q.2)`, so a spatial subtraction-kernel hypothesis is the natural level.
findings.fix: State the kernel constructor with `(hg : IsPositiveDefiniteKernel fun v w : V => g (v - w))` and no `[StarAddMonoid V]`/`hstar`; then make the current `IsPositiveDefinite g` form a derived convenience theorem using `hg.isPositiveDefiniteKernel_sub hstar`. Apply the same change to `isSemigroupGroupPD_mul_time_spatial_of_kernel_and_continuous` at line 101.
findings.issue: `isSemigroupGroupPD_mul_time_spatial_of_kernel` and its continuous packaged version only generalize the time side to a kernel; the spatial side still requires `[StarAddMonoid V]`, `IsPositiveDefinite g`, and `hstar`, although the proof only needs `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel fun v w => g (v - w)`.

summary: The kernel-supplied product constructor is still over-specialized on the spatial factor. It should accept the spatial subtraction kernel directly and derive the `IsPositiveDefinite`/negation-star convenience forms from that.
